module_map.responsibility: Application orchestrator: entry point for fetch, sort, and display pipeline
File metadata collection and assembly. Transforms filesystem paths into Meta structs containing all displayable properties (permissions, size, date, owner, file_type, symlink, git_status, etc.)
Rendering: converts Meta to displayable output using grid/tree layouts. Formats columns, applies colors/icons, handles hyperlinks, padding/alignment.
Column configuration: defines which metadata blocks (permission, user, group, size, date, name, inode, git_status, context) are displayed and in what order
Command-line and config flags affecting behavior: display layout (grid/tree), sorting, icons, colors, permissions, date formats, hyperlinks
ANSI color styling and theme application. Maps elements (filename, permission, etc.) to colors based on theme (dark/light) and file attributes
Icon resolution: determines which icon to display for a file based on extension, name, file_type, and icon theme (Nerd, Unicode, ASCII)
Theme configuration storage and application: loads icon and color themes from config files or defaults
Sorting implementation: defines sort orders (size, date, name, version, extension, inode) and applies sorters to Meta collections
Git repository querying and caching: determines git status (added, modified, deleted, untracked) for files within a git repository

data_flow.description: Stat filesystem and assemble all properties into a single Meta struct via specialized submodules (permissions.rs, date.rs, owner.rs, size.rs, filetype.rs, symlink.rs, etc.)
types_produced: Meta { name, path, permissions_or_attributes, date, owner, file_type, size, symlink, indicator, inode, links, access_control, git_status, ... }

data_flow.description: If directory and not at recursion limit, calls Meta::from_path on each child entry, building content: Option<Vec<Meta>>
If --git flag set, populate GitFileStatus on each Meta.git_status field by querying git repository
If sort flags set, apply one or more sort functions to Meta vector in-place (size, date, name, version, extension, inode)
For each Meta, determine icon from extension (name.rs calls Icons::get_icon), name, or filetype using icon theme (Nerd/Unicode/ASCII)
For each Meta and each display column (name, permission, size, etc.), resolve ANSI color codes based on file attributes and color theme (dark/light)
types_produced: ColoredString (path, permission, size, date, owner, etc. - each colored)

data_flow.description: Convert each Meta into a row of displayable strings. Calls colorize_using_path for each block (permission, owner, group, size, date, name, git_status). Applies padding, truncation, hyperlinks.
types_produced: Vec<String> (one per block column); each element is a ColoredString or formatted value

data_flow.key_function: display::grid(metas, flags, colors, icons, ...) or display::tree(...)
data_flow.description: Assemble cells into final output: grid uses term_grid to calculate columns/alignment; tree recursively formats with indentation and tree characters (├, └). Both call get_output for each Meta.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Core data producer: all file metadata originates here. Depended on by core (fetch), display (rendering), sort (sorting criteria), color (styling), icon (icon resolution), flags (to configure Meta::from_path). No external dependencies (leaf module in dependency DAG).

Core consumer and formatter: takes Meta from core/meta, uses color/icon/theme for styling, respects flags/blocks for layout, produces final output. Central hub where all metadata converges into user-visible form.

Configuration hub: defines output structure (which columns). Depended on by display (which columns to render), core (to configure Meta collection), color (to apply colors per block), icons (block visibility). Centralizes column-related decisions.

change_proposal.title: Add SHA-256 checksum display column
files_to_modify: src/meta/mod.rs - Add `checksum: Option<String>` field to Meta struct
src/meta/ - Create new file `checksum.rs` with `Checksum` struct and from_path logic
src/flags/blocks.rs - Add `Checksum` variant to `Block` enum; extend `get_header()` match; extend `configure_from()` to parse 'checksum' block
src/display.rs - Extend `get_output()` match to handle `Block::Checksum`; add checksum calculation/caching logic
src/theme/color.rs - Add color mapping for `Elem::Checksum` (e.g., cyan for checksums)
src/color.rs - Add `Checksum` variant to `Elem` enum if not already present
src/flags/color.rs - Map checksum element to default color (optional, if color customization desired)
new_types: Checksum struct { value: String } in meta/checksum.rs
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Existing patterns in meta/size.rs and meta/date.rs: (1) Define a struct with a single public field or tuple. (2) Implement From<&Metadata> to compute from filesystem. (3) Implement Display trait for string formatting. (4) In display.rs get_output(), match the Block variant and call the render/colorize methods. (5) In blocks.rs, add variant to Block enum, extend get_header() and configure_from() to recognize the new block name. (6) In color.rs, add Elem::Checksum and map to a color in the theme.
change_proposal.integration_point: Meta::from_path() calls submodule functions (e.g., size::Size::from()) to populate each field. Add `checksum: Checksum::from(&metadata)` there. In display::get_output(), add a match arm for Block::Checksum that renders checksum using the colorize_using_path() pattern. Checksum field appears in the block layout order specified by Blocks (e.g., --blocks permission,checksum,size,name).
risks: Performance: Computing SHA-256 for every file on large directory trees will be CPU-intensive and block display. Mitigate: (a) Lazy-compute only when checksum block is requested; (b) Cache checksums in ~/.config/lsd/checksum.cache keyed by (path, mtime, size); (c) Use rayon for parallel hashing.
Disk I/O: Reading entire file for hash is slow on large files. Mitigate: (a) Only hash regular files, skip directories/symlinks; (b) Option to use fast heuristics (mtime+size) instead of full hash.
Breaking change risk (low): Adding optional field to Meta is backward-compatible. New block name must not conflict with existing Block variants.
Windows compatibility: Ensure Checksum::from() works on Windows (likely fine, since hash algorithm is OS-agnostic, but test on Windows targets).

tool_usage.purpose: Obtain file tree with LOC/function/class counts for all modules
Extract Core struct, main functions (fetch, sort, display), and import list
Extract key display functions (grid, tree, get_output) and dependencies
Extract Meta struct definition and module exports
Extract Block enum, header mapping, and configuration logic
Trace metadata collection entry point: callers (fetch), callees (all meta submodules)
Trace output rendering: callers (grid/tree display), callees (color, format, render submodules)
Identify all cross-module imports in display.rs
List all metadata types in meta/ module

summary.architecture: LSD is a columnar file lister structured as an ETL pipeline: (1) Extract metadata via Meta::from_path() and recursive traversal; (2) Transform via optional sorting and git-status enrichment; (3) Load into display grid/tree layout with colors and icons. Core orchestrates; meta assembles properties; display renders; flags/blocks control columns; color/icon/theme provide styling.
summary.data_flow_sequence: paths → Meta::from_path() [stat + submodules] → Meta[] → sort(optional) → git-enrichment(optional) → display::get_output() [colors+icons] → grid/tree layout → final String
summary.key_integration_points: Meta struct (holds all file attributes), Block enum (defines columns), Colors theme (applies ANSI codes), Icons theme (resolves glyphs), display::get_output() (merges all data into a row)
summary.hub_modules: meta/ (data source), display/ (data sink + formatter), flags/blocks (configuration schema)
summary.change_impact: Adding checksum requires: (1) new field in Meta, (2) computation in meta/checksum.rs via from_path pattern, (3) new Block variant in blocks.rs, (4) rendering logic in display.rs get_output(), (5) color mapping in color.rs Elem enum. Low risk if follows existing patterns; main risk is performance on large files.
